sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.46 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: a) We call a variety of groups a limit variety\/ if it cannot be defined by finitely many laws, while each of its proper subvarieties has a finite basis of identities. It follows from Zorn's lemma that every variety that has no finite basis of identities contains a limit subvariety. Give explicitly (by means of identities or by a generating group) at least one limit variety.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
